channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: CMID clarifies migrant remittances are not reverse, highlights economic contributions
item.description: The Centre for Migration and Inclusive Development (CMID) in Kerala has refuted Chief Minister V.D. Satheesan's remarks suggesting that remittances sent by migrant workers are 'reverse'. CMID executiv...

item.title: B12 Deficiency Risk for Patients on Omeprazole and Metformin
item.description: A pharmacist warns that individuals taking omeprazole or metformin should monitor for symptoms of vitamin B12 deficiency, which can develop slowly and go unnoticed. Common signs include fatigue, weakn...

item.title: Rubio Threatens to Address Trump Soccer Scandal at NATO Summit
item.description: U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio has escalated tensions over President Donald Trump's alleged interference in a soccer player's suspension case by threatening to bring the issue up at an upcoming N...

item.title: Lainey Wilson Reflects on Her Country Music Journey and Inspirations
item.description: Lainey Wilson, a rising country music star, discusses her career journey, inspirations, and the challenges of success in the industry. The article highlights her early life in a small Louisiana town, ...

item.title: Home Affairs Minister Assures Tanzanians of Safety Ahead of July 7 Protests
item.description: Home Affairs Minister Patrobas Katambi has assured Tanzanians that the country remains peaceful and secure, stating there is no security threat despite reports of planned demonstrations on July 7, 202...

item.title: Rivers Monarch Hails Police Move to Remove Illegal Checkpoints, Boosts Economy
item.description: The Eze Oha Evo III of Evo Kingdom, King Leslie N. Eke, praised Rivers State Commissioner of Police CP Olugbenga Adepoju for eliminating illegal checkpoints and roadblocks, which improved traffic flow...

item.title: Analyzing the world's longest golf course through photographic techniques
item.description: The article explores the art of capturing the world's longest golf course through photography, as analyzed by Guardian Australia's photograph and picture editor Carly Early. June's edition features a ...

item.title: Reform UK leader Nigel Farage under fire for financial ties to convicted fraudster
item.description: Nigel Farage, leader of the anti-immigration Reform UK party, is facing mounting scrutiny over allegations of accepting undeclared financial gifts from a convicted fraudster. The controversy has led t...

item.title: World Bank Study Confirms Success of South Africa's Special Economic Zones Programme
item.description: A World Bank report has confirmed the success of South Africa's Special Economic Zones (SEZs) programme, which has generated R14.8 billion in revenue and created over 30,000 jobs. Minister Parks Tau w...

item.title: Maine Senate nominee addresses sexual misconduct allegations and political fallout
item.description: Graham Platner, the Democratic nominee for Maine's U.S. Senate seat, responded to recent allegations of coercing a woman into unwanted sexual activity by stating he is 'taking the time to reflect on t...

item.title: Actor teases intense battles and emotional moments in House of the Dragon's season 3
item.description: The article discusses Gretchen Fullido's report about the cast of 'House of the Dragon' hinting at epic confrontations and emotional turmoil in the upcoming third season. While the text includes promo...

item.title: Farage's Potential Fall and Its Impact on Labour's Future
item.description: The article examines the potential consequences of Nigel Farage's political downfall due to funding scandals, highlighting how his departure could reshape Reform UK and create opportunities for Andy B...

item.title: Maine Senate Candidate Graham Platner Accused of Sexual Assault
item.description: Maine Senate candidate Graham Platner has been accused of sexual assault by Jenny Racicot, a 41-year-old Maine resident. According to Politico, Racicot detailed the alleged incident in three interview...

item.title: Alexa and Akira's Word Hunt Game: A Closer Look at Their Contest
item.description: The article discusses a word-hunt game between Alexa Ilacad and Akira Morishita, highlighting Alexa's unexpected victory. It mentions the game's context within the 'Hanapin Mo Ako Challenge' and refer...

item.title: Steam Machine User Reports Temporary GPU Failure Resolved
item.description: A Steam Machine owner reported experiencing a GPU-related error dubbed the 'Red Line of Death' shortly after the device's launch. The issue occurred during a system update, causing the machine to beco...

item.title: WATU Credit's Smartphone Financing Program Boosts Digital Inclusion in Tanzania
item.description: WATU Credit, a Tanzanian smartphone financing company, has achieved a milestone of serving over one million customers through its affordable device financing service. Launched five years ago, the prog...

item.title: Suspect arrested in Basilan couple's murder case
item.description: The Philippine National Police (PNP) arrested a suspect in the murder of a couple in Basilan, Philippines. Rubber tappers Saturani Bancil and his wife Muhayyara Samsawi were shot in the head at a gras...

item.title: Centre Appoints New Chiefs for BPR&D, NCRB, and SVPNPA
item.description: The Indian government has appointed Alok Kumar Mittal, a 1993 batch IPS officer of Haryana cadre, as Director General of the Bureau of Police Research & Development (BPRD). Mittal, currently serving a...

item.title: Jasmine Paolini advances to Wimbledon quarter-finals after defeating Alexandra Eala
item.description: Italian 13th seed Jasmine Paolini secured her first Grand Slam quarter-final appearance since 2024 after a hard-fought three-set victory over rising star Alexandra Eala at Wimbledon. The match, played...

item.title: Analyst Highlights Strong REIT Performance and Future Outlook Amid Rising Returns
item.description: The article discusses the strong performance of Real Estate Investment Trusts (REITs) in Canada, driven by rising return expectations and analyst optimism. CIBC analyst Dean Wilkinson predicts continu...
